world.title: Diplomats try to arrange second round of US-Iran talks
world.description: US President Donald Trump said a second round of talks could happen "over the next two days," telling the New York Post the negotiations could be held again in Pakistan.

world.description: However, it isn't clear Tehran would submit to US President Donald Trump's terms anytime soon.

world.title: Cyprus tops EU in social media use among young people
world.description: In 2025, 98.25% of individuals aged 16-29 in Cyprus reported using social networking platforms, placing the country at the top among the 19 EU member states with available data.

world.title: On a question of morality, the US and Canada are oceans apart. Why?
world.description: Researchers asked people from different countries to rank the morality and ethics of their fellow citizens. The results could offer insights into how communities can rebuild trust.

world.title: Iran talks 2.0 may happen in next two days in Pak, says Trump. Then praises Munir
world.description: Trump's latest remarks come as tensions between the United States and Iran escalate following the American blockade of Iranian ports, with Tehran threatening regional strikes in response. The showdown over the Strait of Hormuz risks reigniting hostilities and deepening the regional war's economic fallout.

business.title: Mounties arrested 10 people in incident aboard plane in Vancouver

science.title: BMJ retracts most of a special issue for ‘compromised’ peer review and ‘improbable device use’
science.description: BMJ’s Journal of Medical Genetics has retracted the bulk of a seven-year-old special issue for an “irreparably compromised” review process and “improbable device use.”  Of the eight papers in the 2019 special issue, seven were retracted, including an editorial that “almost exclusively” referred to the other now-retracted papers, according to a statement from the journal. …

science.title: Unlocking Secrets of Human Development: How Early Nerve Cell Choices Shape the Peripheral Nervous System
science.description: The peripheral nerve network begins to take shape long before birth. This crucial stage of human development can’t be monitored directly, but by examining genetic clues that linger in adult cells, scientists have now gained surprising insights into the developmental origins of the peripheral nervous system.

science.title: Unlocking the secret of where Earth’s missing lead went
science.description: A study by Nanyang Technological University, Singapore suggests Earth’s missing lead could be deep in the mantle, with implications for understanding how other planets evolve chemically.

science.title: NASA shares photos of an extraordinary event seen by astronauts
science.description: Astronauts captured a stunning and rare lightning event called "red sprites" from Earth orbit that could impact radio, climate, and safety.

science.title: Prep for next Artemis mission begins with launch pad’s Crawler-Transport
science.description: Artemis III prep begins with returning the mobile launcher to Kennedy Space Center’s Vehicle Assembly Building using the Crawler-Transporter. This comes shortly after Artemis II returned humans to the moon for the first time in half a century.

science.title: Here's What Scientists Found When They Cooked Up Their Own 'Mercury Rocks'
science.description: Sulfur on Mercury was as important to the planet's formation as oxygen was to Earth's evolution, the study suggests.
